commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: lexify
repository.description: Headless, plugin-driven Rich Text Editor built on Lexical

commit.message:
chore: bump to v0.9.0

Comprehensive stress testing release:
- 15 bugs found and fixed across rendering, parsing, scroll sync,
  image URI resolution, CRLF handling, nav panel, and cross-module interfaces
- 306 new tests (409→715), covering extreme tables/lists/code/images,
  viewport culling, height estimation accuracy, and inline formatting
- 62KB mega-stress.md test asset for manual testing
- AGENTS.md updated to include rustdown-md crate

Bugs fixed:
1. render_ordered_list overflow near u64::MAX (saturating_add)
2. strengthen_color premultiplied alpha violation
3. Nav click snap in SideBySide mode (sync feedback loop)
4. Stale sync state on mode transitions
5. Scroll lost on Preview→SideBySide switch
6. CRLF normalization corrupted multi-byte UTF-8 (é→Ã¶)
7. Nav panel expanded_hash non-deterministic (HashSet iteration order)
8. Fence validation accepted backticks in info string
9. Code block height over-counted trailing newlines
10. Image URL missing slash between base URI and path
11. Blockquote min-width mismatch (estimation 40px vs render 0px)
12. Stale search cache after document load (edit_seq reset)
13. Fence/heading indentation over-acceptance (4+ spaces)
14. Empty heading ordinal mismatch (render vs nav)
15. Format trailing whitespace in pseudo-fences

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add 20 final edge case and integration stress tests

- Hash collision robustness: single-byte diffs, trailing whitespace,
  CRLF vs LF differences all produce distinct hashes
- heading_y edge cases: empty cum_y, 1000-heading lookup, no-heading doc
- Tight loop stability: 10000 parse+height cycles with no drift
- Extreme parameters: 1px width, 0.1pt font size, finite positive heights
- Pure-block-type documents: 1000 paragraphs, 500 code blocks, 200 tables,
  300 lists, 100 blockquotes, 500 breaks, 200 images
- Stress generator validation: all 6 generators at 1KB produce valid markdown

715 tests total (240 GUI + 475 md lib), all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Render markdown tables as box-drawing tables in assistant messages

Detects markdown table syntax (|header|, |---|, |cell|) in assistant
message content, parses it, and renders as aligned box-drawing tables
with dim borders, bold cyan headers, and white cell text. Non-table
content passes through wordWrap unchanged. Only applied to
assistant.message content — reasoning and user messages unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(network): fix 4G relay reservation dispatch (v0.1.33)

- Remove relay_server from client MetaverseBehaviour — clients should
  not relay for others; this eliminates the relay::server 10s idle timer
  that was keeping connections alive just long enough to mask the real bug

- Defer listen_via_relay from ConnectionEstablished to Identify::Received
  — Identify fires after libp2p deduplicates multiple transports (TCP+WS),
  so the reservation request goes to the surviving connection's handler,
  not the one that gets closed by dedup

- Add circuit_registered: HashSet<PeerId> to track which relay peers have
  an active circuit reservation. Guards against duplicate listen_via_relay
  calls and cleared on ConnectionClosed (num_established == 0)

- Increase idle_connection_timeout from 60s to 300s as safety net

- Update connect_to_bootstrap re-registration to filter by circuit_registered

Root cause: libp2p-relay 0.21.1 priv_client.rs uses .first() to select
which connection handler receives a listen_on dispatch. Both TCP and WS
circuit listen_on calls landed on the TCP handler (connected first).
When TCP closed due to dedup, both reservations were lost. WS handler
had reservation=None so connection_keep_alive() returned false.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix 5 critical UX bugs: tab switching, address bar, navigation, snapshots

1. Tab reload on switch: Add .id(activeTab.id) on WebViewContainer only
   (not entire BrowserView) so pooled webviews are properly swapped via
   makeUIView instead of updateUIView trying to load new URL in old webview

2. Address bar non-interactive: Remove .onTapGesture on root ZStack that
   was intercepting taps before they reached AddressBarView's Text onTap.
   Add 50ms delay on @FocusState change for reliable TextField focusing.

3. Back/forward buttons always inactive: Add coord.parent = self in
   updateUIView so coordinator always has latest closures. Add initial
   state fire (canGoBack/canGoForward/URL/title) via DispatchQueue.main
   for pooled webviews since KVO only fires on value changes.

4. Tab preview snapshots missing: Capture snapshot after every page load
   (1s delay for rendering) in addition to existing switchToTab capture.

5. Fix Share Extension version mismatch (1.0 → 1.0.0)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: align cross-module interfaces for fences, headings, and nav scroll

Three interface mismatches fixed:

1. markdown_fence.rs + highlight.rs: accepted arbitrarily-indented fences
   and headings (trim_start strips all whitespace). CommonMark limits ATX
   heading and code fence indentation to 0-3 spaces. Lines with 4+ spaces
   are indented code blocks, not headings/fences. This caused highlight.rs
   to color code blocks as headings, and format.rs to preserve trailing
   whitespace inside pseudo-fences that pulldown_cmark never recognizes.

2. render.rs heading_y vs nav_outline: heading_y counted ALL Block::Heading
   variants (including empty ones like '# '), but nav_outline::extract_headings
   skips empty headings. This ordinal mismatch caused preview_nav_scroll_y
   to navigate to wrong positions when documents contained empty headings.
   Fixed heading_y to skip empty headings, aligning with nav_outline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  Sources header row to be inline with the Patient: NM_007294.4 line

  cards share the same computed width

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci(prow,tekton,pipelines): add pingcap-inc active-active triggers and revert ticdc env OCI host change (#4291)

## Summary
- Add active-active branch CI triggers for `pingcap-inc/tidb` and
`pingcap-inc/ticdc`:
  - update release presubmit configs in Prow
- add Tekton `git-push` triggers under
`tekton/v1/triggers/triggers/env-gcp/pingcap-inc/{tidb,ticdc}`
  - register new trigger files in env-gcp `kustomization.yaml`
- Update 7 `pipelines/pingcap/ticdc/latest/*` integration pipelines to
revert PR changes in `environment.OCI_ARTIFACT_HOST`:
- restore `environment` host to fixed public hub value
(`us-docker.pkg.dev/pingcap-testing-account/hub`)
- keep per-step `OCI_ARTIFACT_HOST=...` override for TiDB download, so
`pingcap-inc` still uses internal mirror for TiDB artifacts

## Changed Files
- `pipelines/pingcap/ticdc/latest/*` (7 pipeline files)
- `prow-jobs/pingcap-inc/tidb/release-presubmits.yaml`
- `prow-jobs/pingcap-inc/ticdc/release-presubmits.yaml`
- `tekton/v1/triggers/triggers/env-gcp/kustomization.yaml`
- `tekton/v1/triggers/triggers/env-gcp/pingcap-inc/tidb/git-push.yaml`
- `tekton/v1/triggers/triggers/env-gcp/pingcap-inc/ticdc/git-push.yaml`

## Notes
- This PR now reflects the current branch state after reverting
unrelated `tidb` release-8.5 pipeline changes.

---------

commit.message:
feat: add state vector / basis-state probability table to circuit navigator

Add StateVectorPanel component inside GroverStepNavigator:
- Scrollable table: |state⟩ | DNA | magnitude √p | prob |ψ|² | bar
- √p decomposition line showing dominant states by magnitude
- Target state (marker) highlighted amber with 🎯 badge + left border
- Phase-sign disclaimer: √prob is always positive; oracle phase-flip
  sign (-|target⟩) is not recoverable from probabilities alone
- DNA decoding: 2 bits per nucleotide (00=A, 01=C, 10=G, 11=T)
- Renders per Grover step alongside existing AmplitudeChart

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: extract DEFAULT_DENY_RESULT and ruleToResult() in policy-engine (#62)

* refactor: extract DEFAULT_DENY_RESULT and ruleToResult() in policy-engine

The same two EvaluationResult object literals were repeated three times
each across evaluateRulesUnscoped, evaluateRulesForRole, and
evaluateRulesForMultiPaths. Extract them into a module-level constant
(DEFAULT_DENY_RESULT) and a helper (ruleToResult) so the default-deny
message and the rule-to-result mapping live in a single place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Running under IronCurtain

* Update src/trusted-process/policy-engine.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Align strategy names with registry keys for correct lastScraped lookup

Strategy constructors used short names ('checking', 'portfolios',
'foreignCurrency') but the registry and strategySync use the full keys
('checking-accounts', 'investment-portfolios', 'foreign-currency').
This caused getScraperOptionsForStrategy(this.name) to miss the
stored lastScraped date and fall back to 6 months on every scrape.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Include choice_map in ask_user responses for deterministic choice values (#27)

* Include choice_map in ask_user responses for deterministic choice values

The LLM agent was mapping 'Wait for another approver' to 'wait_approver'
instead of 'wait_for_approver', causing an unrecognized choice error.
Rather than relying on the LLM to look up SKILL.md, every ask_user
response now includes a choice_map dictionary that maps each display
text to its exact choice value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix 'Re-run the failed jobs' display text to match ChoiceValueMap entry

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Log warning when choice has no ChoiceValueMap entry

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review comments: centralize SerializeAction, comprehensive choice map test, SKILL.md fallback entries

- Add SerializeAction() helper that auto-attaches choice_map for all ask_user
  actions, replacing 15 direct serialize calls and 3 separate AttachChoiceMap calls
- Add AllActionBuilders_EveryChoiceString_ExistsInChoiceValueMap test covering
  terminal, comment, CI failure, waiting-comment, and error fallback flows
- Add missing ChoiceValueMap entry for 'I'll handle them myself'
- Add 4 missing SKILL.md fallback entries: Run a new build, Stop monitoring,
  Resolve the conflict (rebase), Apply the recommendation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ui): UX audit — 20 usability improvements

- Fix keepalive row layout bug in BT config table (orphaned rows)
- Fix muted button color in dark theme via CSS class instead of inline style
- Fix mobile action buttons layout (flex-row, full-width)
- Move Docs/GitHub links out of <h1> into proper nav below title
- Add 'Released' badge on device card header
- Group badge: filter raw hex UUID hashes, only show human-readable names
- Group filter dropdown: same UUID filter applied
- Group volume slider: initialise from average of active devices on load
- Re-anchors count: add tooltip explaining the term
- BT audio sinks in Diagnostics: render each as <code> block (was comma string)
- Auto-refresh button: use CSS class .auto-on instead of hardcoded #10b981
- Paired devices list: replace RSSI-only names with 'Unknown device'
- Config dirty-state: dot indicator + beforeunload warning on unsaved changes
- Keyboard shortcuts: move hints into header (right column), remove footer hint
- Advanced settings: show count of non-default fields in button label
- Scan progress: animated CSS spinner instead of static text
- Re-pair button: add confirm() dialog before executing
- Release/Reclaim buttons: add descriptive title attributes
- Toast errors: display for 6 s instead of 3 s
- BT table: horizontal scroll shadow via CSS gradient on .bt-table-wrap
- Progress time font-size: 10px → 12px for readability

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: panel scale mismatch and invisible scatter on project load

Panel scale: save sceneUnitsPerMeter per panel during serialize and
use it during restore via _createPanelObject override parameter.
Previously panels were recreated with DEFAULT_SCENE_UNITS_PER_METER
(0.2) instead of the calibrated value, causing wrong sizes.

Scatter: set finalOnly=false when loading saved projects so base
scatter (green dots) renders immediately via 1.5s fallback timer.
Previously finalOnly=true meant dots only appeared after full shading
raycasts completed, which could take very long or hang silently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
